ledger.paperTitle: AtlasRAG · Long-Context Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Scientific QA

events.after: Attention Is All You Need — sequence transduction models based on attention, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

events.before: Dense retrieval over scientific text has been studied extensively. Specter and SciNCL learn paper-level embeddings, while ColBERTv2 generalises to fine-grained passage retrieval.
events.after: Dense retrieval over scientific text is well-studied: SPECTER and SciNCL learn paper-level embeddings, ColBERTv2 generalises to passage-level.

sources.label: Khattab & Zaharia · ColBERTv2
sources.quote: ColBERTv2 trains a single late-interaction model that we use as a stronger retrieval baseline.

sources.label: Cohan et al. · SPECTER
sources.quote: SPECTER produces paper-level embeddings using the citation graph as supervision.

sources.label: Vaswani et al. · Attention Is All You Need
sources.quote: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

events.after: We argue for a middle path: long-context reading lets the reader reason across surviving evidence after retrieval narrows the haystack.

events.prompt: make the experimental claim more rigorous
events.before: AtlasRAG achieves 71.3% exact-match accuracy compared to a strong dense baseline.
events.after: AtlasRAG achieves 71.3% (95% CI 70.1–72.5) exact-match accuracy compared to a strong dense baseline of 59.9%.

sources.label: (internal · evaluation results table 2)
sources.quote: Table 2 reports per-seed exact-match scores. AtlasRAG: 71.3% (σ=0.6).

unsupportedClaims: claim that the 11.4-point delta is statistically significant
